title: GPT-2
description: A decoder-only Transformer language model from OpenAI that made large-scale next-token pretraining a mainstream reference point for modern language models.
openingSummary: GPT-2 matters because it turned a plain decoder-only Transformer trained with next-token prediction into a widely recognized example of zero-shot language transfer, making the model itself a useful doorway into transformer blocks, causal attention, tokenization, and decoding.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: GPT-2 is a research language model released by OpenAI in 2019. It is a text-only decoder-only Transformer: tokenized text goes in, hidden states move through repeated causal attention and feed-forward blocks, and the model emits logits for the next token. It is best treated as a canonical checkpoint page because many later families keep the same broad recipe while changing scale, data, or post-training.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: The input is tokenized text, not raw characters. GPT-2 reads a prefix, updates one hidden state per token position, and produces a probability distribution over the next token. Repeating that next-token prediction step yields longer text outputs, which is why GPT-2 sits naturally beside tokenization, next-token prediction, and autoregressive generation pages.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: GPT-2 uses the Transformer family in a decoder-only layout. Each layer applies causal attention so later positions cannot peek at future tokens, then a feed-forward network refines the per-token representation before the language-model head scores the next token. The architecture graph below treats GPT-2 itself as the root so readers can move outward to Transformer, causal attention, tokenization, and next-token prediction without leaving the page context.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: The key module story is not exotic specialization but the baseline recipe. GPT-2 is a useful reference point because standard attention and multi-head attention are still easy to spot, so later variants such as grouped-query attention can be explained as changes to a familiar decoder stack.

training.title: Training
training.body: Public GPT-2 summaries describe the model as large-scale unsupervised pretraining on web text with a next-token objective. In this atlas slice, the structured training-regime, dataset, and paper relationship slots are still optional, so the page renders explicit unavailable states instead of implying richer metadata than the registry currently has.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: GPT-2 is historically important, but it is not a modern chat or reasoning model. It is best used here as a clean architectural reference: a decoder-only Transformer with causal attention, fixed tokenization, and a straightforward next-token loop. That makes it easier to compare newer checkpoints without starting from a heavily post-trained product model.

architectureGraph.alt: Architecture graph showing GPT-2 as the root with tokenization, decoder blocks, causal attention, feed-forward layers, and next-token prediction as the main parts.
architectureGraph.caption: GPT-2 as a decoder-only Transformer: tokenization feeds a causal decoder stack, which feeds the next-token prediction head.

gpt2.label: GPT-2
gpt2.summary: A decoder-only Transformer checkpoint used here as a canonical language-model reference.

tokenizer.label: Tokenization
tokenizer.summary: Maps raw text into token IDs before the decoder stack runs.

decoderStack.label: Decoder stack
decoderStack.summary: Repeated Transformer blocks update one hidden state per token position.

causalAttention.label: Causal attention
causalAttention.summary: The mask lets each token read only the current prefix, not future tokens.

ffn.label: Feed-forward network
ffn.summary: The per-token MLP stage that refines features after attention.

lmHead.label: Next-token prediction
lmHead.summary: The language-model head turns the final hidden state into logits over the next token.
